items.claim: Felipe Franco is durable and dangerous.
items.quote: He's pretty durable and he's kind of dangerous.

items.claim: Felipe Franco has good control and pressure on the ground.
items.quote: If he does get you to the ground, he's got good control on top, good pressure.

items.claim: Levi Rodriguez Jr. has solid takedown defense.
Sakyouong Co is a striker who uses takedowns as a secondary tool.
items.quote: I promise you, he's a striker. He comes forward, he's stiff as hell, he throws hard, and he's got a ton of power, but he will also work in some takedowns.

items.claim: Sakyouong Co's stance leaves him vulnerable to being taken down.
items.quote: He has a weird stance that leaves him open to be taken down

items.claim: Jean Paul Lebanoni's takedowns are not very good.
items.quote: The takedowns are not very good, and that is the problem here.

items.claim: Jean Paul Lebanoni is susceptible to being snapped back by a simple jab.
items.quote: He can be snapped back with a simple jab.

items.claim: Jose Delgado's takedown defense is only average.
items.quote: Takedown defense is just okay. It's not great. It's not horrendous. It's just okay.

items.claim: Austin Bashi is a high-level wrestler and grappler with BJJ credentials.
items.quote: Austin Bashi's very good wrestler, very good wrestler and a very good grappler. I think he's like a ghee and a no ghee BJJ world champion

items.claim: Tommy McMillan is highly durable and aggressive at range.
items.quote: we know he's tough as hell. We know he's a gamer. We know this dude is an absolute savage dog at range when he's striking.
